title: Prosaposin maintains lipid homeostasis in dopamine neurons and counteracts experimental parkinsonism in rodents
type1_summary: All inspected files are structured Excel source-data workbooks organized by figure or region, with tabular quantitative outputs suitable for direct replotting.
type2_summary: No file shows clear raw instrument output, proprietary binary format, microscopy image stacks, or other unprocessed primary data.
classification_notes: The paper clearly generates raw microscopy, MALDI-MSI, electrophysiology, and behavioral data, but the provided files are not raw outputs; they are curated source-data spreadsheets. Therefore, despite the paper's raw-data potential, the file evidence dominates here and supports Type 1 classification. Rule-based prior suggests hidden Type 2 evidence; review recommended.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper includes figure-level quantified lipid/lipidomics source data organized by brain region, consistent with source-data tables for plotted results.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Excel workbook with 5 region-specific sheets (16m CPu, Ctx, CC, Hipp, SN+VTA), each with headers and 12 columns x 182 rows. Structured tabular data is directly replot-ready.
file_classifications.reasoning: This is a clean, figure-oriented spreadsheet with named brain-region sheets and tabular measurements. It matches source-data style rather than raw instrument output, and can be plotted with minimal processing.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Region-specific sheets; tabular measurements for 16m CPu, Ctx, CC, Hipp, SN+VTA

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper presents processed lipid statistics and comparisons derived from MALDI-MSI, which are typical source-data outputs for figure panels.

file_classifications.reasoning: The file contains processed statistical summaries rather than raw spectra or images. It is organized as analysis-ready tabular data and is directly replot-ready.
file_classifications.key_columns_or_structure: Lipids, Discovery?, P value, Mean of KO, Mean of WT, Difference, SE of difference, t ratio, df, q value, -log10(q value)

file_classifications.ambiguity: minor: some sheets lack explicit headers, but overall structure remains tabular and figure-oriented.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies prosaposin (PSAP), a lysosomal/neurotrophic protein, in Parkinson’s disease using human postmortem tissue, biofluids, conditional mouse knockouts, MALDI mass spectrometry imaging, and viral/encapsulated-cell gene delivery. The main finding is that PSAP is reduced in dopaminergic neurons and circulating biofluids in PD, dopaminergic PSAP loss disrupts lipid homeostasis and behavior in mice, and PSAP overexpression protects against α-synuclein- and 6-OHDA-induced nigrostriatal degeneration.
measurement_types: ELISA / immunoassay for PSAP, PGRN, PSAP-PGRN complex
Behavioral assays (open field, light-dark box, pole test, beam traversal, rotarod, forced swim, apomorphine rotation)
MALDI mass spectrometry imaging (dual polarity spatial lipidomics)
Principal component analysis / volcano plots / ANOVA on lipidomics

figures.description: Immunofluorescent staining of PSAP and PGRN in human substantia nigra pars compacta neurons; quantification shows PSAP reduced in TH-positive neurons in PD while PGRN is unchanged.
Scatter plots correlating CSF/plasma PSAP and PGRN with each other and with clinical scores (UPDRS-III, BDI-II).
Validation of conditional PSAP deletion in DA or serotonin neurons by FISH and immunostaining; neurotransmitter measurements and PSAP/PGRN protein trajectories in cPSAPDAT mice.
Behavioral testing timeline and behavioral phenotyping of cPSAPDAT and cPSAPSERT mice.
MALDI-MSI spatial lipidomics in cPSAPDAT mouse brain showing PCA separation, volcano plots, ion images, and altered cardiolipins/sphingolipids.
figures.data_type: mass spectrometry image + volcano plot + PCA + ion images

figures.description: Spatial lipidomics in cPSAPSERT dorsal raphe nucleus showing increased gangliosides and altered tryptophan metabolism, plus neurotransmitter-related ion images and quantification.
figures.data_type: mass spectrometry image + volcano plot + ion images + bar plots

figures.description: AAV-α-syn and AAV-PSAP rescue experiment in cPSAPDAT and WT mice, including apomorphine rotation behavior, TH/DAT staining, and p-Ser129 α-syn quantification.
6-OHDA lesion experiment in WT mice with AAV-PSAP or AAV-GFP, showing behavioral protection and preservation of TH/DAT.
Encapsulated cell biodelivery (ECB) experiment in rats delivering PSAP, PGRN, or PSAP-PGRN against AAV-α-syn toxicity; device secretion was validated by ELISA and chromatography.
figures.data_type: assay plots / secretion quantification / in vivo outcome plots

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports collection of raw microscopy images (immunofluorescence, IHC, FISH), raw HPLC chromatograms/quantified neurotransmitter measurements, raw MALDI-MSI ion images from brain sections, ELISA/flow cytometry measurements, electrophysiology recordings of striatal LTP, and behavioral readouts from mouse/rat assays. The text does not provide file formats or repository links in the excerpt, but the experiments clearly generate primary instrument data.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper presents processed quantifications such as mean fluorescence intensity, densitometry, neurotransmitter concentrations, behavioral summary metrics, correlation scatter plots, PCA score plots, volcano plots, fold-change plots, and region-specific lipid abundance summaries derived from MALDI-MSI and other assays.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The provided excerpt does not include a formal data availability statement or repository accession. The paper text mainly describes experimental methods and figure outputs.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are implied to come from microscopy, HPLC, MALDI-MSI, ELISA, flow cytometry, electrophysiology, and behavioral testing, but the excerpt does not specify file formats, raw file deposition, or public access to instrument files.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include quantified fluorescence intensities, neurotransmitter concentrations, densitometry values, behavioral summary statistics, correlation analyses, PCA/ANOVA outputs, and lipid fold-changes/q-values from spatial lipidomics.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific quantitative data are clearly shown in the plots and captions, implying source-data style figure quantifications. The excerpt does not explicitly mention 'source data' files, but the figures are heavily quantified and likely accompanied by figure source data in the journal package.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned in the excerpt.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Many figures are based on explicit numerical quantifications (scatter plots, bar graphs, PCA, volcano plots, fold changes), so the plotted values are replot-ready if source tables are available. However, the excerpt does not provide the underlying tables or raw files directly.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is multimodal and mixed across human clinical samples, mouse/rat tissue, and imaging mass spectrometry. The strongest dataset semantics are for processed figure data rather than raw files. MALDI-MSI is especially important: the paper uses dual-polarity spatial lipidomics with PCA and volcano plots, indicating substantial downstream processing from raw spectra/images to annotated lipid features and region-wise statistics.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper describes primary measurements from microscopy, HPLC, MALDI-MSI, ELISA, flow cytometry, electrophysiology, and behavioral testing, all of which generate raw instrument/observation data.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The figures present quantified fluorescence intensities, densitometry, neurotransmitter concentrations, PCA/volcano plots, and lipid fold-changes, which are processed figure-level data.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper clearly implies both primary raw measurements and processed figure data, but the excerpt does not explicitly state deposition of raw files or source-data tables.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: No explicit data availability wording is included in the provided text excerpt.
priority_modalities: MALDI-MSI raw spectra and ion images
classification_prior.review_notes: Do not confuse raw imaging/spectra with processed lipidomics outputs. The paper’s main quantitative claims rely on processed values extracted from images and MSI, not on raw files alone. The excerpt does not provide enough evidence for scripts or public raw-data deposition, so those should remain unknown unless the full data availability section is available.
